item.title: Lamp not working, 30th April
item.description:
Street lamp on the bend towards to top of Hollingworth Rd. it working again. Seems pretty temperamental. It’s pretty dark on the bend making it potentially a road safety issue. Think it’s already been fixed but it’s out again.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hollingworth Road, Petts Wood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No lights in various lampposts in st Mary cray rec, 30th April
item.description:
Various lampposts in st Mary cray recreational ground don’t work, or flicker, it’s been like this for ages now

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Park Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shopping trolley dumped, 30th April
item.description:
Sweet to the belly jamaican stall in the centre of Bexleyheath next to the clock tower have dumped a shopping trolley in that little alley which belong to our building . It is not the first time that people see them dumping rubbish as well using our bins . When I confronted him today he reply fk off to me . I told him to remove that trolley and rubbish otherwise I would have called the police but he laugh and turn around and left for his stall

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4-5, Market Place, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish left, 30th April
item.description:
Rubbish left outside

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Peacock Street, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large flytipping - repeated & regular, 30th April
item.description:
Flytipping again... out the back of the ground flat garden of property associated with garden gate pictured. Cat litter/container, lots of wood, bags of garden/construction waste etc. Began last couple of days and growing. Starting to block pavement

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Powerscroft Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish on street and over flowing x, 30th April
item.description:
This house consistently has rubbish over flowing please can you intervene and a longer term solution.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Waverley Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Some one is putting thier rubbish in our bins without putting them in trash covers and beer bottles in rubbish bins., 30th April
item.description:
This is the 5th time i am facing this yesterday i put every thing in black covers and i sorted every thing today they put beer bottles and other rubbish things.I have small kids they will play in the back yard .some times the people are keeping beer bottles on the ground too.I am conserned about kids.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Eltham Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped bedding, 30th April
item.description:
There is some bedding which has been dumped on the pavement on the odd numbers side of the road. It’s been there about two weeks.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bowness Road, Catford South, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Corrugated cement fibre sheets that may contain asbestos, 30th April
item.description:
I am writing here to follow up on a matter previously reported in November 2025 concerning suspected asbestos-containing corrugated roofing sheets left at the entrance to a public park within your area. Despite this being reported several months ago, the material remains in situ and has not been removed. The sheets appear to be cement fibre roofing panels, which may contain asbestos. As I highlighted in my earlier report, there was a clear risk that routine grass cutting would disturb and spread debris from these materials. This has now occurred, with mowing equipment dispersing fragments across the surrounding area. This situation presents an ongoing environmental hazard and a potential risk to both public health and animals using the park. It is particularly concerning given the prolonged period of inaction following the initial notification. I would ask that this matter be treated with urgency and that appropriate steps be taken to assess and safely remove the material without delay. If the issue is not resolved within seven days, I will have no option but to escalate the matter to the Environment Agency, including providing a record of prior correspondence regarding the lack of action to date.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
